description_long: This object consists of a diverse collection of stylized flowers arranged in a grid, each with distinct colors including green, red, blue, brown, and purple. The flowers display a variety of petal shapes, from broad and rounded to narrow and pointed, and are organized in rows with some showing more open, spiky forms while others are compact and layered. The petals are arranged radially, with some flowers exhibiting multiple whorls or layers, creating a sense of depth and complexity. The leaves, where visible, are generally lanceolate or linear, radiating outward from the base of the flowers. The overall appearance is vibrant and decorative, suitable for urban parks, gardens, or landscaped sidewalks. The object appears lightweight and is likely composed of synthetic materials, mimicking the look of real flowers for ornamental use.
description: A vibrant, multi-colored collection of stylized flowers with varied petal shapes and radial arrangements, suitable for decorative urban environments. The flowers are organized in a grid, displaying a range of open and compact forms.
description_view_0: A frontal view showing the full grid of flowers, highlighting the diversity in color and petal arrangement, with all flowers clearly visible.
description_view_1: A side view emphasizing the height and layering of the flowers, with the petal shapes and leaf arrangements more pronounced from this angle.
description_view_2: A rear view displaying the backs of the flowers, revealing the structure of the petals and the radial symmetry from behind.
description_view_3: An alternate side view showing the opposite profile, with the color variations and petal layering still visible but from a different perspective.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial and whorled configuration, with some flowers displaying multiple concentric whorls and others having a single radial layer. There is clear radial symmetry, and the petals often overlap or are layered for a fuller appearance.
